title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
summary: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.
title_es: OpenAI y Broadcom presentan chip Jalapeño para inferencia de LLMs
bajada: OpenAI y Broadcom presentaron Jalapeño, un chip de IA personalizado diseñado específicamente para mejorar el rendimiento e eficiencia en la inferencia de modelos de lenguaje. Este desarrollo es relevante para developers que buscan optimizar costos y latencia en despliegues de producción.
bajada_en: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño, a custom AI chip specifically designed to improve performance and efficiency in LLM inference. This development matters for developers looking to optimize costs and latency in production deployments.

title: Qualcomm Buys Buzzy Chip Startup Modular for Nearly $4 Billion
summary: Modular, one of the most promising chip software startups of the AI era, heads for a multibillion-dollar exit.
title_es: Qualcomm adquiere Modular por casi 4 mil millones de dólares
bajada: Qualcomm completó la adquisición de Modular, una startup promisoria en software de chips para IA, por casi 4 mil millones de dólares. Esta operación refleja la carrera acelerada por controlar la infraestructura de IA y optimización de hardware.
bajada_en: Qualcomm completed its acquisition of Modular, a promising AI chip software startup, for nearly $4 billion. This deal reflects the accelerating race to control AI infrastructure and hardware optimization.

title: ProductJun 23, 2026Introducing Claude TagClaude Tag is a new way for teams to work with Claude.
summary: Product Introducing Claude Tag Jun 23, 2026 Claude Tag is a new way for teams to work with Claude. We’re starting on Slack, which Claude can join as a team member. Grant Claude access to selected channels, and connect it to whichever tools, data—and even codebases—you choose. Then, anyone in the channel can tag @Claude in, and delegate tasks to it while they focus on other work. Claude builds context by remembering relevant information from the channels it’s in, and can plan out tasks to complete in the future. We see Claude Tag as the beginning of an evolution of Claude Code: it makes the model even more proactive, and it works better with a full team. Tagging @Claude is now one of the main ways we get things done at Anthropic. Today, 65% of our product team’s code is created by our internal version of Claude Tag. The same pattern is now spreading well beyond engineering—we’re tagging Claude to chase down product metrics and data, work through support tickets, or even help find the root cause of tricky bugs. We’re launching Claude Tag on Slack, since it’s a natural home for collaborative work between teams and AI, and where much of Anthropic’s day-to-day work already happens. It’s available today in beta for Claude Enterprise and Team customers. Our goal is to expand where it’s available more widely, so that teams can tag @Claude in the many other places they work. Working with @Claude If you’ve worked with Claude Code or Cowork before, Claude Tag will feel familiar. Tag @Claude with a request in simple terms and it’ll break its task down into stages and then work through them in turn, using the tools it has access to. Once it’s done, it’ll respond in a Slack thread with what it’s created. But tagging Claude comes with a few new advantages: @Claude is multiplayer. Within a given Slack channel, there’s one Claude that interacts with everyone. This means that anyone can see what it’s working on, and can pick up the conversation from where the last person left off. Th
title_es: Anthropic lanza Claude Tag para colaboración en equipo en Slack
bajada: Anthropic presentó Claude Tag, una herramienta que permite equipos colaborar con Claude directamente en Slack. El 65% del código del equipo de producto de Anthropic ya se crea usando esta función, demostrando potencial como forma de trabajo en equipos.
bajada_en: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, enabling teams to collaborate with Claude directly in Slack. 65% of Anthropic's product team's code is already created using this feature, demonstrating its potential as a team workflow tool.

Across Amazon OpenSearch and Amazon EC2, NVIDIA AI infrastructure is giving enterprises more practical paths to deploy […]

bajada_en: NVIDIA and AWS announced a collaboration to deploy AI systems in production at scale, addressing latency, vector search, and cost efficiency challenges. The initiative provides practical paths for enterprises to adopt AI in real-world environments.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time

bajada_en: Claude Tag in Slack isn't just about productivity—it's a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows. Anthropic aims to become essential infrastructure for business operations.

title: RIFT-Bench: Dynamic Red-teaming For Agentic AI Systems
summary:
arXiv:2606.23927v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic AI systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are rapidly evolving into autonomous decision-making systems, exposing attack vectors beyond those of traditional LLM vulnerabilities. Existing security evaluations are often tied to specific implementations or domains, limiting unified comparison across heterogeneous systems. To address this gap, we introduce RIFT-Bench, a graph representation-driven methodology for dynamic red-teaming that enables unified evaluations across diverse agentic architectures. Building on a novel hierarchical representation, RIFT-Bench operates in two automated phases: Discovery, which extracts system structure, and Scanning, which deploys adaptive adversarial attacks and produces a comprehensive evaluation report. It evaluates the examined system itself, leveraging a broad set of dynamically adaptable adversarial probes across diverse attack vectors and objectives. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed evaluation pipeline across 45 agentic systems spanning a diverse range of implementations, showing that the approach generalizes effectively to heterogeneous agentic architectures. Beyond systems and attacks, RIFT-Bench also supports direct evaluation of mitigation strategies. These key capabilities make RIFT-Bench a scalable foundation for security evaluation of agentic AI systems.
title_es: RIFT-Bench: evaluación dinámica de seguridad para sistemas agénticos
bajada: Investigadores presentaron RIFT-Bench, metodología para evaluar seguridad de sistemas IA agénticos mediante red-teaming dinámico. Herramienta crítica para developers que despliegan agentes autónomos en producción.
bajada_en: Researchers introduced RIFT-Bench, a methodology for evaluating agentic AI system security through dynamic red-teaming. Critical tool for developers deploying autonomous agents in production.

title: DeepSWE: new benchmark looking at how well today's frontier models can actually write code [R]
summary: DeepSWE delivers four advances over existing public benchmarks:Contamination free: Tasks are written from scratch, not adapted from existing commits or PRs, so no model has seen the solution during pretraining.High diversity: Tasks span a broad pool of 91 repositories across 5 languages.Real-world complexity: Prompts are ~half the length of SWE-bench Pro's, yet solutions require 5.5x more code and ~2x more output tokens.Reliable verification: Verifiers are hand-written to test software behavior rather than implementation details.The result is a benchmark that reflects how today's frontier coding agents actually perform in software engineering work.https://preview.redd.it/lacvagyr159h1.png?width=1373&format=png&auto=webp&s=6514340a15d51d7f03da733f08fb3f6a302cac75It's open-source:https://github.com/datacurve-ai/deep-swesubmitted by/u/we_are_mammals[link][comments]
title_es: DeepSWE: benchmark realista para coding con modelos frontier
bajada: DeepSWE es nuevo benchmark que evalúa qué tan bien escriben código los modelos frontier actuales con tareas reales complejas. Benchmark libre de contaminación y más desafiante que evaluaciones previas.
bajada_en: DeepSWE is a new benchmark evaluating how well frontier models write code with real complex tasks. The benchmark is free of contamination and more challenging than previous evaluations.

title: How Businesses Are Building Specialized AI They Can Trust
summary: Companies are asking how to build specialized AI that fits with the way their workflows actually run.  The first wave of enterprise AI was about access. Companies experimented with new frontier and open models, ran pilots and explored how AI can help.  Now, specialized agents — systems of models that can reason, use tools and […]

bajada_en: NVIDIA published guidance on building specialized AI agents that fit real enterprise workflows. The proposal marks a transition from exploratory AI toward task-oriented autonomous systems.

title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
summary: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale. In many cases, though, the relevant information is blocked or unstructured, which limits its use by AI models.  To understand this challenge, consider the foundation of the web itself. The web was not designed…

bajada: Empresas enfrentan reto de acceder a datos web a escala para entrenar sistemas IA, pues mucha información está bloqueada o desestructurada. Emerge una nueva capa de infraestructura para resolver este cuello de botella.
bajada_en: Enterprises struggle to access web-scale data for AI systems, as much information is blocked or unstructured. A new infrastructure layer is emerging to solve this bottleneck.

title: I compiled LLM inference pricing across 7 providers — the caching numbers are surprising(spreadsheet included) [R]
summary: I've been comparing GPU/LLM providers for a side project and ended up with way too many browser tabs and spreadsheets.So I decided to pull the public pricing data into one sheet and compare it side by side.A quick disclaimer: this isnot benchmark data. I didn't run latency tests or throughput measurements. Everything comes from public pricing pages and APIs (OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Together AI, Fireworks, Groq, etc.).The spreadsheet currently tracks:Input/output token pricingContext windowsCached input pricing (where available)Supported modelsProvider-specific pricing differencesThe thing that surprised me most was caching.For example, when looking at DeepSeek V4 Pro pricing across providers, cached input costs vary dramatically. In some cases a cache hit is tens of times cheaper than a cache miss.That made me realize that if you're running:Agents with large system promptsRAG pipelines with reusable contextMulti-turn conversationsRepeated prompt templates...the "headline" token price can be a lot less important than the caching policy.A few other interesting things I noticed:The same model can vary by multiple times in cost depending on provider.Some providers expose caching clearly, while others barely document it.Model availability and context windows aren't always consistent across providers.It's surprisingly hard to find all of this information in one place.A few things I haven't figured out how to compare yet:Real throughput (tokens/sec)Cold-start / queue timesWhether providers are serving FP16, FP8, quantized variants, etc.Egress/network costsReliability/uptimeI'm curious how others evaluate providers.When you're choosing between OpenRouter, Together, Fireworks, Groq, DeepSeek, etc., what metrics actually matter to you beyond token pricing?https://preview.redd.it/4vj50mvhu79h1.png?width=1615&format=png&auto=webp&s=6c6c084927f83bfdadb5ed8e4378f520a1da6766Am I missing any important data points that should be included in a v2?submitted by/u/Technomadlyf[link][comme
title_es: Análisis de precios de inferencia LLM: caching cambia la ecuación
bajada: Análisis comparativo de precios entre 7 proveedores de LLM revela que caching de prompts es factor más importante que precio nominal. Para agentes, RAG y prompts reutilizables, políticas de caching varían hasta 10x en costo.
bajada_en: Comparative pricing analysis across 7 LLM providers reveals prompt caching is more important than headline pricing. For agents, RAG, and reusable prompts, caching policies vary up to 10x in cost.

title: EXPO-SQL: Execution-based Clause-level Policy Optimization for Text-to-SQL
summary:
arXiv:2606.23693v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-to-SQL enables users to query databases using natural language by generating executable SQL queries. Recent methods have increasingly adopted Large Language Models based reinforcement learning (RL) to leverage execution feedback for training. However, existing RL methods assign uniform query-level rewards to all clauses in a SQL query, treating correct and incorrect clauses equally. This coarse-grained reward design leads to insufficient learning signals for correct SQL generation. To address this issue, we propose EXPO-SQL (EXecution-based clause-level Policy Optimization for Text-to-SQL) which provides fine-grained supervision through clause-level rewards. To assign clause-level rewards, our method identifies erroneous clauses by analyzing execution results, including error messages and clause-wise incremental execution. Experiments on widely-used Text-to-SQL benchmarks demonstrate that EXPO-SQL significantly outperforms existing supervised fine-tuning, prompting, and RL-based methods through fine-grained clause-level learning. Our code is available at https://github. com/jhn25/EXPO-SQL.
title_es: EXPO-SQL: optimización de políticas para Text-to-SQL con recompensas
bajada: Nuevo método EXPO-SQL mejora traducción de lenguaje natural a SQL usando recompensas a nivel de cláusula en lugar de nivel de query. Avance significativo en RL aplicado a generación de código SQL.
bajada_en: New EXPO-SQL method improves natural language to SQL translation using clause-level rewards instead of query-level rewards. Significant advance in RL applied to SQL code generation.

title: Stripe, Anthropic and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
summary: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now, the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new…
title_es: Stripe y empresas IA financian investigación sobre infecciones respiratorias
bajada: Stripe, Anthropic y OpenAI co-financian una iniciativa de investigación para prevenir infecciones respiratorias. Es un ejemplo de aplicación de IA más allá del software tradicional hacia problemas biomédicos reales.
bajada_en: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are co-funding a research initiative to prevent respiratory infections. It exemplifies AI application beyond traditional software toward real biomedical challenges.

title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
summary: OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday. Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it's designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI […]
title_es: OpenAI y Broadcom presentan Jalapeño, chip de inferencia para LLMs
bajada: OpenAI reveló su primer procesador de IA diseñado específicamente para inferencia de modelos de lenguaje, desarrollado en asociación con Broadcom. El chip ASIC Jalapeño busca optimizar el costo y rendimiento de los servidores de IA, marcando la entrada de OpenAI en el mercado de semiconductores.
bajada_en: OpenAI revealed its first AI processor specifically designed for LLM inference, developed in partnership with Broadcom. The Jalapeño ASIC chip aims to optimize cost and performance for AI servers, marking OpenAI's entry into the semiconductor market.

title: Computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash

bajada_en: Google introduced 'computer use' capability in Gemini 3.5 Flash, enabling the model to directly interact with user interfaces. This feature opens new possibilities for automating tasks that require navigation and application control.

Part of this is a reimagined canvas that's now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials "together in […]

bajada_en: Figma introduced new AI capabilities including motion graphics and shader tools, plus a redesigned canvas for full-stack development. These updates aim to automate repetitive tasks and improve collaboration between designers and developers.

title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals

bajada_en: Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel, renowned Google researchers, moved to Anthropic, continuing a trend of talent leaving Google for competitors. This move reflects shifts in Google's research hierarchy.

title: The Trump White House Is Over Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei

bajada: La administración Trump sustituyó a Dario Amodei, CEO de Anthropic, con Tom Brown en reuniones de alto nivel con la Casa Blanca. El cambio refleja tensiones políticas entre el gobierno y líderes de empresas de IA.
bajada_en: The Trump White House replaced Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei with Tom Brown in high-level meetings. The change reflects political tensions between the government and AI company leaders.

title: I Met With China’s Top AI Experts. They’re Freaking Out, Too
summary: The AI arms race between China and the US has researchers on both sides worried about a “Chernobyl moment.”
title_es: Expertos de IA en China advierten sobre escalada con Estados Unidos
bajada: Investigadores de IA chinos expresan preocupación por la carrera armamentística entre China y EE.UU., temiendo consecuencias catastróficas similares a Chernóbil. La conversación refleja anxiedad global sobre la competencia descontrolada en IA.
bajada_en: Chinese AI experts express concern about the AI arms race between China and the US, fearing catastrophic consequences similar to Chernobyl. The conversation reflects global anxiety about uncontrolled competition in AI.

title: For Most of the World, Open-Source AI Is the Only Way Forward
summary: Article URL:https://techstrong.ai/articles/for-most-of-the-world-open-source-ai-is-the-only-way-forward/Comments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48660839Points: 187# Comments: 128
title_es: IA open-source es la única opción viable para la mayoría del mundo
bajada: Un análisis sostiene que para la mayoría de países en desarrollo, el software de IA open-source es la única ruta viable ante dominancia de modelos propietarios occidentales. El argumento destaca la importancia de democratizar el acceso a IA.
bajada_en: An analysis argues that for most developing countries, open-source AI software is the only viable path given the dominance of Western proprietary models. The argument highlights the importance of democratizing AI access.

title: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel

bajada_en: NVIDIA launched NeMo AutoModel, a tool that optimizes the fine-tuning process for transformer models. This solution enables developers to significantly accelerate training without sacrificing quality.

bajada_en: RubyLLM launched, a framework enabling Ruby developers to access multiple AI providers through a consistent interface. It simplifies integration of models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google and others in Ruby applications.

title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood

bajada_en: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, saw its stock plummet after reporting narrower gross margins than expected in its first earnings report as a public company. The CEO argues investor expectations were misunderstood.

title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
summary: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.
title_es: Empresas racionan gastos de IA ante abuso de presupuestos
bajada: Las compañías implementan controles para prevenir que empleados agoten presupuestos de IA en tareas menores, marcando el fin de la era de gasto ilimitado. La tendencia muestra la necesidad de optimizar costos mientras el uso de IA se generaliza.
bajada_en: Companies are implementing controls to prevent employees from exhausting AI budgets on minor tasks, marking the end of unlimited spending. The trend shows the need to optimize costs as AI adoption becomes mainstream.

title: Introducing the FFASR Leaderboard: Benchmarking ASR in the Real World

bajada_en: Hugging Face launched FFASR Leaderboard, an evaluation platform for automatic speech recognition models in real-world conditions. The tool aims to bridge the gap between academic benchmarks and production performance.

title: How to Opt Out of Google Search’s New AI Data Training Feature
summary: Google’s Search history update stores media uploads from your interactions, like images used in reverse image searches, for training its AI models.
title_es: Cómo desactivar el entrenamiento de datos de Google Search
bajada: Google recopila datos multimedia de búsquedas (como imágenes en búsqueda inversa) para entrenar sus modelos de IA. Los usuarios ahora pueden optar por no participar en esta práctica, aunque la configuración no es visible por defecto.
bajada_en: Google collects media data from searches (such as images in reverse image searches) to train its AI models. Users can now opt out of this practice, though the setting is not visible by default.

title: A24 Knows You’re Mad About the Google AI Collab

bajada_en: Google DeepMind invested $75 million in film studio A24, sparking backlash from indie film fans concerned about AI influence on creativity. The move reflects AI companies' expansion into creative industries.
